corporate_owner: Bosch Home Comfort Group (acquired JCI HVAC August 2025)

brands.name: York
brands.notes: Primary JCI residential brand, now Bosch-owned

brands.name: Coleman
brands.notes: Badge variant, identical Johnson Controls hardware

brands.name: Luxaire
brands.notes: Badge variant, identical Johnson Controls hardware

brands.name: Champion
brands.notes: Badge variant, identical Johnson Controls hardware

brands.name: Guardian
brands.notes: Badge variant, identical Johnson Controls hardware

brands.name: Fraser-Johnston
brands.notes: Badge variant, identical Johnson Controls hardware

brands.name: Evcon
brands.notes: Badge variant, identical Johnson Controls hardware

brands.name: Bosch
brands.notes: Bosch-branded IDS inverter equipment, different architecture from JCI brands

structural_overview: As of 2026, the field-installed base is still mostly Johnson Controls-era residential hardware even though Bosch completed the JCI HVAC acquisition on August 1, 2025. The practical platform split is: (1) legacy JCI 24V furnace IFC flash-code boards used on TG8S/TG9S/TM8Y and badge-equivalent Coleman/Luxaire/Champion units; (2) modulating/variable-capacity YP9C/CP9C/LP9C/TP9C boards with amber status + red fault codes and pressure transducer as primary flow sensor; (3) outdoor communicating-capable dual-LED boards on some split AC/heat pump units; (4) Affinity/Hx communicating ecosystem (display/storage layer only, not a separate code dictionary); (5) Bosch IDS inverter split systems with alphanumeric fault codes closer to ductless equipment logic. No publicly documented new Bosch-era residential furnace fault-code family has emerged yet for 2026 equipment.

field_quirks.quirk: York flame current is much higher than most brands
field_quirks.applies_to: All JCI legacy and YP9C boards
field_quirks.reason: Normal flame current is approximately 3.7 µA DC — significantly higher than the 0.5-1.5 µA most techs anchor to on other brands. TG9S family low-flame warning starts at 1.5 µA with lockout at 0.1 µA; TM8Y family warning at 0.28 µA with lockout at 0.16 µA. A tech using another brand's rule of thumb can misjudge whether York flame signal is healthy.

field_quirks.quirk: Cross-generation code conflicts between TG9S/TM8Y and YP9C are severe
field_quirks.reason: Code 4 means 'main limit open' on TG9S/TM8Y but 'high limit OR defective temp sensor OR open 24V fuse' on YP9C. Code 5 is rollout/limit-circuit-open on TG9S/TM8Y but 'rollout OR condensate pressure switch' on YP9C. Code 6 is 'pressure switch reopened' on legacy boards but 'gas valve communication error' on YP9C. A tech memorizing legacy codes will misdiagnose YP9C.

field_quirks.quirk: YP9C uses a pressure transducer as primary flow sensor, not just a switch
field_quirks.reason: The pressure transducer is the primary flow sensor. The pressure switch can be open under some burner-lit conditions. This makes 'bad pressure switch' a common false diagnosis if the transducer/condensate/venting side is ignored.

field_quirks.quirk: Bosch IDS inverter uses a completely different code architecture
field_quirks.applies_to: Bosch IDS 2.0 and newer R-454B IDS families
field_quirks.reason: Alphanumeric codes like C3, E4, E5, H8, P1/F1, L0-L9 — much closer to ductless/inverter logic than York furnace flash logic. Techs trained on York furnaces need to mentally reset for IDS equipment. Some limited-condition families (P**, U**, F**, C**, d**) are followed by two digits showing compressor frequency in Hz while the unit continues to run in a limited state.

field_quirks.quirk: Bosch acquisition transition — most installed equipment is still JCI-era
field_quirks.applies_to: All York/Coleman/Luxaire residential equipment installed through 2026
field_quirks.reason: Bosch completed the JCI HVAC acquisition August 1, 2025. However, the field-installed base is still predominantly Johnson Controls-era hardware. No publicly documented new Bosch-era residential furnace fault-code family replaces the older JCI dictionaries yet. Troubleshoot existing equipment using JCI documentation; only use Bosch IDS architecture if the equipment is specifically Bosch IDS.

platforms.platform_name: Legacy JCI 24V Furnace IFC Flash-Code Boards
platforms.platform_description: Classic Johnson Controls residential furnace ignition control with single-color LED flash codes, soft/hard lockout logic, and LAST ERROR history function. Used on TG8S, TG9S, TM8Y and sibling Coleman/Luxaire/Champion/Guardian/Fraser-Johnston/Evcon legacy furnaces.
applies_to_models: Coleman/Luxaire/Champion equivalent single-stage and two-stage models
platforms.visual_identification: Single LED that flashes slow green for normal, slow amber for heat call, numeric red for faults, continuous amber for low flame. Used on TG8S/TG9S/TM8Y single-stage and two-stage furnaces.
platforms.platform_notes: Board stores up to 5 errors. Repeat-trigger lockouts use 1-hour soft lockout plus power-cycle hard lockout logic depending on code. Slow green flash indicates no thermostat call (normal). Slow amber flash indicates normal operation with call for heat.

codes.meaning: Normal operation, no thermostat call
codes.escalation: None

codes.meaning: Normal operation with call for heat
diagnostic_path: No action — normal status during heat call
codes.escalation: None

codes.meaning: Twinning error, incorrect 24V phasing, or no power to twinned unit
root_causes: Loss of power to secondary twinned furnace
codes.escalation: If twinning wiring and power are correct, board fault is possible.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when twinning issue is resolved.

codes.meaning: Low flame sense current
diagnostic_path: Clean flame rod and measure flame current (normal ~3.7 µA)
Verify burner carryover and flame pattern
TG9S family: warning at 1.5 µA, lockout at 0.1 µA
TM8Y family: warning at 0.28 µA, lockout at 0.16 µA
codes.escalation: Repeated low flame after cleaning and grounding points to combustion quality or board sensitivity.
codes.reset_behavior: Status indicator during operation; does not itself lockout until flame drops below lockout threshold.

codes.meaning: No board power or board failure
codes.escalation: If power is confirmed at board and LED remains off, board failure is likely.

codes.meaning: Possible control failure or open fuse (family-dependent)
diagnostic_path: Consult model-specific manual for steady LED behavior
codes.escalation: Board-level fault indicator if fuse and power check out.

codes.meaning: Flame sensed when there is no call for heat
diagnostic_path: Verify no call for heat and gas valve should be closed
codes.escalation: Leaking gas valve is a safety concern. If no external cause, gas valve or control fault becomes likely.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch closed when it should be open
diagnostic_path: Verify switch state before inducer starts
Verify inducer is not being held on improperly
codes.escalation: If switch and inducer are normal but board still sees closed, board fault is more likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when startup proofing is normal.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch open when it should be closed
diagnostic_path: Use manometer to verify vent pressure (TM8Y)
codes.escalation: Repeated with verified draft pressure points to switch or control.
codes.reset_behavior: If recurring during call, can advance to lockout logic.

codes.meaning: Main limit switch opened during call for heat
diagnostic_path: Check filter, static pressure, airflow first
Verify blower speed and motor operation
Verify firing rate and limit circuit
codes.escalation: If limit does not re-close in time, escalates to 11 red. Repeated trips five times trigger 1-hour soft lockout.
codes.reset_behavior: 1-hour soft lockout after repeated trips; otherwise clears when limit recloses.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT with YP9C: On YP9C boards, Code 4 can mean high limit open, defective temp sensor, OR open 24V fuse — broader than TG9S/TM8Y. Verify board before trusting this meaning.

codes.meaning: Rollout / limit-circuit-open hard safety condition
diagnostic_path: Inspect burners, heat exchanger, inducer BEFORE resetting
codes.escalation: Repeated rollout or non-resettable rollout is a hard combustion safety problem.
codes.reset_behavior: Hard lockout; power must be cycled after fault is corrected.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT with YP9C: On YP9C boards, Code 5 can also indicate condensate pressure switch open. Check condensate drainage.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch opened four times during the heat call
codes.escalation: Pattern indicates marginal draft or switch.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT with YP9C: On YP9C boards, Code 6 means gas valve communication error — completely different. Verify board.

codes.meaning: No ignition after three trials
root_causes: Gas off or low gas pressure
diagnostic_path: Verify gas valve switch and gas supply
codes.escalation: Repeated trials exhausted; retry after lockout.

codes.meaning: Flame lost five times / too many recycles during heating cycle
codes.escalation: Repeated flame loss indicates combustion instability.

codes.meaning: Reversed line polarity, grounding problem, or reversed low-voltage transformer wires
diagnostic_path: Inspect flame probe to chassis short
codes.escalation: Ignition sequence inhibited until corrected.

codes.meaning: Gas valve energized with no call for heat
diagnostic_path: Replace valve/control only after verifying circuit
codes.escalation: Ignition sequence inhibited while fault exists; safety concern.
codes.reset_behavior: Inducer and blower run, ignition inhibited.

codes.meaning: Limit circuit remained open more than 5 minutes (main blower failure indicator)
root_causes: Failed blower motor or blower wheel
diagnostic_path: Verify blower actually runs and moves air
codes.escalation: Strong blower failure indicator on this family.
codes.reset_behavior: Hard lockout; power cycle required after correction.

codes.meaning: Second-stage/high pressure switch open with call for second-stage heating
diagnostic_path: Use manometer to verify vent pressure
codes.escalation: Follows active fault logic; clears when proof is correct.

platforms.platform_description: Johnson Controls modulating furnace board family with amber status displays, red fault codes, pressure transducer as primary flow sensor, gas-valve communication logic, and ID-plug logic. Used on YP9C, CP9C, LP9C, TP9C and sibling modulating families.
platforms.visual_identification: Modulating furnace board with separate amber (status) and red (fault) LED indicators. Pressure transducer is the primary flow sensor, not a simple switch.
platforms.platform_notes: Materially different from legacy JCI platform. Adds amber operating/status displays, pressure transducer as primary flow sensor, gas-valve communication logic, and ID-plug logic. Different fault meanings for the same flash numbers compared to TG9S/TM8Y. Normal flame current ~3.7 µA, low-flame warning at 1.5 µA, lockout at 0.1 µA.

codes.meaning: Cooling call normal
codes.escalation: None

codes.meaning: Heating call normal
codes.escalation: None

codes.meaning: Burner on after thermostat satisfied / end of heat cycle
diagnostic_path: No action — normal cycle end status
codes.escalation: None

codes.meaning: Reduced heating capacity from circulating-air restriction
codes.escalation: Persistent condition may trigger limit-related fault.

codes.meaning: Reduced heating capacity from combustion-air/vent restriction or clogged condensate drain
codes.escalation: Persistent condition may trigger pressure-switch fault.

codes.meaning: Heat-pump heating normal
diagnostic_path: No action — normal heat pump operation
codes.escalation: None

codes.meaning: Low flame-sense current
diagnostic_path: Clean flame rod and measure current (normal ~3.7 µA)
codes.escalation: Warning at 1.5 µA, lockout at 0.1 µA.

codes.meaning: Flame present with no power to gas valve
codes.escalation: Leaking valve is a safety concern.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch closed with inducer pressure below setpoint (switch closed when it should be open)
diagnostic_path: Compare transducer reading to switch behavior
codes.escalation: Transducer/switch disagreement indicates sensor or board issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when startup proofing is normal.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch open with inducer pressure above setpoint (switch open when it should be closed)
codes.escalation: If transducer reads correct but switch doesn't agree, switch or board input issue.

codes.meaning: High-limit switch open, defective temperature sensor, or open 24V fuse
diagnostic_path: Determine which branch is actually open: limit, sensor, or fuse
codes.escalation: Multiple possible causes; isolate before replacing components.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT with TG9S/TM8Y: On legacy boards, Code 4 means ONLY main limit open — narrower meaning. YP9C Code 4 is broader.

codes.meaning: Rollout switch or condensate pressure switch open
root_causes: Blocked condensate drainage causing safety trip
diagnostic_path: Identify which switch is open physically BEFORE troubleshooting
If condensate: inspect drain path and trap
codes.escalation: Rollout is a hard combustion safety problem.
codes.reset_behavior: Hard lockout if rollout; condensate may clear with drain service.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT with TG9S/TM8Y: On legacy boards, Code 5 is rollout/manual-reset safety only — YP9C also includes condensate switch.

codes.meaning: Gas valve communication error
root_causes: Gas valve wiring or communication issue
codes.escalation: Communication issue blocks valve operation entirely.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT with TG9S/TM8Y: On legacy boards, Code 6 means pressure switch reopened — completely different from YP9C gas valve communication meaning.

codes.meaning: Lockout due to no ignition after three tries
codes.escalation: Repeated trials exhausted.

codes.meaning: Lockout due to too many flame recycles (flame lost five times)
codes.escalation: Repeated flame loss indicates combustion instability.

codes.meaning: Reversed line polarity or improper grounding
codes.escalation: Clears when corrected.

codes.meaning: Gas valve circuit shorted
diagnostic_path: If wiring correct, replace gas valve per manual
codes.escalation: Safety concern until resolved.

codes.meaning: Main blower failure (main limit opened and failed to reclose within 5 minutes)
diagnostic_path: Verify blower operates and moves air
codes.escalation: Stronger blower-specific language than legacy boards.

codes.meaning: ID plug absent or not connected properly
codes.escalation: Configuration error until plug is restored.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when plug is properly connected.

codes.meaning: Control fault detected or 115V present without 24V
diagnostic_path: If both present and fault remains, replace board
codes.escalation: Board-level fault if power is confirmed.

platforms.platform_name: JCI Outdoor Split AC/Heat Pump Board with LED1/LED2 Diagnostics
platforms.platform_description: Johnson Controls York-family split outdoor units with communicating-capable control boards (CZF / communicating outdoor control style). Uses red LED1 and green LED2 combinations for fault and status indication.
platforms.visual_identification: Outdoor unit control board with red LED1 and green LED2 combinations. Also has a yellow communication status LED. Used on York-family communicating-capable split AC and heat pump outdoor units.
platforms.platform_notes: Outdoor-board codes, not furnace codes. In communicating applications, faults are also stored in the thermostat. Yellow LED status: fast flash = active communication present, slow flash = board powered without communication. Soft lockout reset by thermostat satisfaction, SYSTEM OFF cycle, or shorting TEST >2 sec after fault removal.

codes.meaning: Control failure
diagnostic_path: Verify board power state and harnesses
codes.escalation: Depends on actual failed condition.
codes.source: JCI outdoor Table 6 operational fault codes

codes.meaning: High-pressure switch fault, not yet in lockout
codes.escalation: Repeated events escalate to lockout.

codes.meaning: System in high-pressure switch lockout
root_causes: Repeated HPS openings within six hours
codes.escalation: Hard lockout if unresolved.
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout; reset by thermostat satisfaction / SYSTEM OFF or shorting TEST >2 sec after fault removal.

codes.meaning: System in low-pressure switch lockout
root_causes: LPS opening >5 sec outside ignore windows
diagnostic_path: Note LPS is ignored during first 2 minutes of compressor run
codes.escalation: Repeated soft lockouts escalate to hard lockout.

codes.meaning: Low voltage below 19.2 VAC preventing further relay outputs
codes.escalation: Higher severity if voltage drops further.

codes.meaning: Low voltage below 16 VAC stopped current relay outputs
codes.escalation: Equipment will not operate until voltage is restored.

codes.meaning: High-pressure switch fault with no communication for compressor operation and Y1/Y2 not energized
root_causes: Abnormal HPS event outside active compressor command
codes.escalation: Indicates wiring or pressure issue outside normal operation.

codes.meaning: Outdoor ambient temperature sensor short
codes.escalation: Replace sensor if shorted.

codes.meaning: Outdoor ambient temperature sensor open
codes.escalation: Replace sensor or repair wiring.

codes.meaning: Compressor contactor miswire
root_causes: Field wiring error to contactor/compressor outputs
diagnostic_path: Verify M / M1 / M2 wiring against diagram
codes.escalation: Safety concern — verify wiring before operating.

codes.meaning: Y2 present without Y1 (thermostat or field-wiring staging error)
codes.escalation: Staging configuration issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Soft-lockout-style reset; two occurrences do not create hard lockout.

platforms.platform_description: Bosch IDS 2.0 and newer inverter ducted split systems (BOVA-BVA). Completely different architecture from York/Coleman flash-code furnaces — uses alphanumeric fault codes similar to ductless/inverter equipment. Newer R-454B families add indoor refrigerant leak detection indications.
platforms.visual_identification: Alphanumeric display (letters + numbers like C3, E4, P1) on Bosch IDS inverter equipment. Completely different from York furnace flash logic. Newer R-454B units add 3/4/8 flash indoor leak-detection indications.
platforms.platform_notes: Alphanumeric code format. Limited-condition families P**, U**, F**, C**, d** are followed by two digits showing current compressor frequency in Hz while unit continues to run in a limited condition. H5 specifically requires a hard restart even though most other abnormal conditions self-clear when the condition goes away. Most newer R-454B products include 3/4/8 flash indoor leak-detection indications.

codes.meaning: T3 sensor not reading correctly in cooling
codes.escalation: Sensor correction required.

codes.meaning: Temperature sensor fault (T3/T4/T5/Th/T3L/Tf)
codes.escalation: Multiple sensors to check — isolate specific sensor.

codes.meaning: High/low voltage protection
codes.escalation: U** is the limited-condition family tied to this protection state.

codes.meaning: Outdoor DC fan motor fault
codes.escalation: Motor replacement likely if electrical and mechanical check good.

codes.meaning: T5 sensor not reading correctly
codes.escalation: Installation error likely; verify diagrams.

codes.meaning: EEPROM IC20 fault
codes.escalation: Board/electronics fault, not refrigerant-side.

codes.meaning: Communication fault in EEPROM IC14 chip
codes.escalation: Board-level fault.

codes.meaning: Pressure transducer fault
codes.escalation: Transducer replacement likely.

codes.meaning: Control board overheated
root_causes: Outdoor motor issue causing board overheat
codes.escalation: Falls under F** limited-condition family.

codes.meaning: High-pressure protection
codes.escalation: One of the most important real-world IDS codes.

codes.meaning: Low-pressure protection
codes.escalation: H5 specifically requires HARD RESTART — does NOT self-clear when condition goes away (unlike most other abnormal conditions).
codes.reset_behavior: H5 requires hard restart; P2 self-clears.

codes.meaning: Compressor over-current protection
codes.escalation: Persistent overcurrent threatens compressor.

codes.meaning: High compressor discharge temperature protection
codes.escalation: Sits in d** limited-condition family before worsening into hard fault.

codes.meaning: Confirmed IDS 2.0 protection code (specific wording requires manual extraction)
root_causes: System protection triggered — specific cause requires Bosch manual consultation
diagnostic_path: Consult Bosch IDS 2.0 service manual for specific P5 procedure
codes.escalation: Part of P** family protection codes.
codes.source: Bosch IDS 2.0 troubleshooting (specific table requires extraction)

codes.meaning: Confirmed IDS 2.0 protection code (specific wording requires manual extraction)
root_causes: System protection triggered — specific cause requires Bosch manual consultation
diagnostic_path: Consult Bosch IDS 2.0 service manual for specific P8 procedure
codes.escalation: Part of P** family protection codes.
codes.source: Bosch IDS 2.0 troubleshooting (specific table requires extraction)

codes.meaning: Confirmed IDS 2.0 protection code (specific wording requires manual extraction)
root_causes: System protection triggered — specific cause requires Bosch manual consultation
diagnostic_path: Consult Bosch IDS 2.0 service manual for specific PH procedure
codes.escalation: Part of P** family protection codes.
codes.source: Bosch IDS 2.0 troubleshooting (specific table requires extraction)

codes.meaning: IPM module protection
codes.escalation: Important inverter-specific family — do NOT treat like a normal low-voltage code.

codes.meaning: Ambient temperature limited condition
codes.escalation: Limited condition, NOT necessarily a hardware failure.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when ambient returns to operating range.

codes.meaning: Defrost mode
diagnostic_path: No action — normal defrost cycle status
codes.escalation: None — this is a status display, not a service fault.
codes.reset_behavior: Status only; clears when defrost ends.

codes.meaning: Refrigerant leak detection indication (R-454B indoor units)
root_causes: Possible refrigerant leak detected by indoor leak detection system
diagnostic_path: Inspect indoor unit for visible leaks
codes.escalation: A2L refrigerant leak requires mitigation procedure.

codes.meaning: Refrigerant leak detection indication (R-454B indoor units)
root_causes: Possible refrigerant leak, sensor problem, or poor connection
codes.escalation: A2L refrigerant leak requires mitigation procedure.

codes.meaning: Refrigerant leak detection indication (R-454B indoor units)
root_causes: Possible refrigerant leak, sensor problem, or poor connection
codes.escalation: A2L refrigerant leak requires mitigation procedure.

communicating_ecosystem.name: Affinity / Hx
communicating_ecosystem.notes: The York-family communicating system uses Hx communicating controls with a bus labeled A+, B-, R, and GND/C. Diagrams show communicating outdoor unit ↔ communicating furnace/air-handler ↔ Hx thermostat. In communicating applications, fault codes are stored in the thermostat. The Hx thermostat/user manual shows Status, Service/SVC, Log, and app Fault notifications, plus dealer-access functions. However, NO separate thermostat-only numeric code dictionary exists analogous to Rheem EcoNet or Bosch IDS. Safest model for the tool: thermostat/app = display/storage/notification/service access layer, equipment board = source of the actual fault dictionary. SSN (Simplified Service Network) does NOT have a separate public numeric fault dictionary — do not build a separate SSN code family.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On TG9S/TM8Y legacy boards, 4 red flashes means ONLY main limit switch open during call for heat. On YP9C modulating boards, 4 red flashes expands to high limit open, defective temperature sensor, OR open 24V fuse. The YP9C meaning is broader — a tech treating it narrowly will miss sensor and fuse causes.
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: Identify furnace model first. TG9S/TM8Y single-stage = legacy meaning (limit only). YP9C/CP9C/LP9C/TP9C modulating = broader meaning (limit OR sensor OR fuse).

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On TG9S/TM8Y legacy boards, 5 red flashes means rollout/limit-circuit-open (usually manual-reset rollout). On YP9C modulating boards, 5 red flashes can be rollout switch OR condensate pressure switch open. YP9C adds condensate safety circuit to this code.
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: Physically identify which switch is open before troubleshooting. On YP9C, check condensate drain path even if rollout switch appears to be the trigger.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On TG9S/TM8Y legacy boards, 6 red flashes means pressure switch reopened four times during heat call (pressure/venting issue). On YP9C modulating boards, 6 red flashes means gas valve communication error (completely different — communication issue between board and gas valve).
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: This is the most severe cross-generation conflict in the York family. Verify furnace model before troubleshooting — legacy interpretation leads tech to inducer/vent, YP9C interpretation leads to gas valve wiring.

electric_heat_notes.conventional_electric_furnace_codes: None verified publicly. Johnson Controls-era York/Coleman residential air handlers and heater kits do not have a meaningful public standalone electric-heat code dictionary. Public air-handler installation manuals are wiring/configuration heavy, not heater-fault-table heavy.
electric_heat_notes.manufactured_home_specifics: Not applicable — York/Coleman brands do not primarily serve manufactured-home market.
